title: Clinical Trial Enrollment Summary
input:
PHASE III CLINICAL TRIAL — PROTOCOL CT-2026-048
Study: Efficacy of NOV-7742 in Type 2 Diabetes Management
Sponsor: Novagen Pharmaceuticals
Site: Stanford Clinical Research Center (Site Lead: Dr. Amara Osei)

ENROLLMENT STATUS AS OF May 8, 2026:

INCLUSION CRITERIA:
  1. Age 30-75
  2. HbA1c between 7.0% and 10.5%
  3. Currently on metformin monotherapy for ≥3 months
  4. BMI ≥25 and ≤42
  5. Willing to attend all follow-up visits

EXCLUSION CRITERIA:
  1. History of DKA in past 12 months
  2. eGFR < 45 mL/min/1.73m²
  3. Chronic liver disease (Child-Pugh B or C)
  4. Taking SGLT2 inhibitors within 30 days of screening
  5. Pregnant or breastfeeding

SCREENING RESULTS (First 85 patients):
  Screened: 85
  Enrolled: 67
  Failed screening: 18
    — HbA1c too low (<7.0%): 5
    — HbA1c too high (>10.5%): 3
    — BMI out of range: 4
    — On excluded medication (SGLT2i): 3
    — DKA history: 2
    — eGFR below threshold: 1
    — Other (liver disease): 0
    — Other (pregnancy): 0

ENROLLED PATIENTS DEMOGRAPHICS:
  Mean age: 58.3 years (SD 9.7)
  Female: 34 (50.7%)
  Male: 33 (49.3%)
  Mean baseline HbA1c: 8.42% (SD 0.89)
  Mean BMI: 33.1 (SD 4.2)
  Mean duration of diabetes: 6.8 years (SD 4.1)
  Baseline metformin dose: mean 2,100 mg/day

ADVERSE EVENTS (reported by 67 enrolled):
  Mild: 23 patients (34.3%)
    — GI upset: 12
    — Headache: 7
    — Fatigue: 4
  Moderate: 8 patients (11.9%)
    — Hypoglycemia: 5
    — Elevated liver enzymes: 3
  Severe: 1 patient (1.5%)
    — DKA (resolved): 1
    — Contributing factors: non-compliance with metformin dose, consumed alcohol during 4th week

DISCONTINuations: 3 patients dropped out
  — Relocated: 1
  — Adverse event (moderate hypoglycemia): 1
  — Lost to follow-up: 1

Remaining enrolled: 64

ground_truth.study_name: Efficacy of NOV-7742 in Type 2 Diabetes Management

severe_details: DKA (resolved) — contributing factors: non-compliance with metformin dose, consumed alcohol during 4th week
